title: Rice and waxy brassica foliar delivery starter scope
citation: Project-authored starter document for FoliarShield-AI retrieval workflow validation; replace with license-compatible public literature before benchmarking.
abstract: FoliarShield-AI starts with rice as an agriculturally relevant cereal leaf and a waxy brassica or cabbage-type leaf as a difficult-to-wet hydrophobic benchmark. Fast assay labels include droplet impact, contact-line motion, spray coverage, retained fluorescent intensity, wash-off, evaporation, and early release. Living-payload assays remain contained and review-gated.

title: Cloaked droplets and liquid-liquid encapsulation starter scope
citation: Project-authored starter document for FoliarShield-AI retrieval workflow validation; replace with license-compatible public literature before benchmarking.
abstract: The formulation search space includes cloaked droplets, liquid-liquid encapsulation, polymer-assisted emulsions, release-trigger materials, and controlled-release shell architectures. The first optimization loop should use nonliving payload labels for retention and release before testing Bacillus-like payload viability under contained conditions.

title: Batched active learning starter scope
citation: Project-authored starter document for FoliarShield-AI retrieval workflow validation; replace with license-compatible public literature before benchmarking.
abstract: FoliarShield-AI uses a batched, tiered discovery loop. Surrogate models estimate outcomes and uncertainty from fast physical assays, then Bayesian optimization proposes the next formulation batch under safety, feasibility, sprayability, material, and responsible-use constraints. The LLM agent retrieves evidence, explains hypotheses, checks constraints, and prepares auditable rationales for human review.
